title: content.rs: bare URL inserted into HTML without entity-encoding
description:
LOCATION: src/content.rs:70-73

FINDING: process_content inserts regex-matched URL text directly into an href attribute and as display text via format!() without calling escape_html_attr or ammonia::clean. The URL regex excludes double-quote and angle brackets, but does not exclude ampersand. A URL containing & (common in query strings) is inserted as literal & into both the href value and the display text. In attribute context, HTML parsers treat &foo; as an entity reference. This corrupts the URL when round-tripped through an HTML parser. More fundamentally, the code pattern of inserting untrusted URL text into raw HTML format strings without escaping is a dangerous invariant that contributors will copy for future features.

IMPACT: URLs with & in query strings are double-encoded inconsistently. If the regex is ever relaxed, or a new format-string call site is added following this pattern, XSS results. Current risk is low due to the regex, but the unsafe pattern will be copied.
close_reason: Fixed: bare URLs in content.rs now use escape_html_attr for href
